answers.answer: Change these values in `shared-config/`, published as `@mento-protocol/config`: it is the source of truth for chain metadata (including chain IDs and explorer metadata), deployment namespaces, token/pool label derivation, thresholds, and shared ABIs. Change `src/` or the JSON inputs, never hand-edit `dist/`; add a cross-reference test, run the config package lint, typecheck, test, and build, and typecheck dashboard, indexer, bridge, and integration-probes consumers whenever an exported shape changes.

evidence.supports: Defines shared-config as the source of truth and states its data-change and consumer-stability rules.
Lists package verification and required consumer typechecks for exported-shape changes.

answers.answer: Use `indexer-envio/README.md` for the current add-contract procedure: add or generate the ABI, update every applicable `config.multichain.*.yaml`, update schema, add the handler under `src/handlers/`, side-effect import it from `src/EventHandlers.ts`, then run package codegen and the stateful-data/UI checklist. `indexer-envio/AGENTS.md` is the package authority for the key-file map and hosted-build mirrors: keep `config/deployment-namespaces.json` synchronized with shared-config, preserve the token metadata mirror, and retain vendored ABIs because hosted Envio builds can run outside the workspace.

evidence.supports: Defines the contracts-package upgrade, namespace update, ABI generation, and typecheck procedure.
Defines the complete new-contract, config, schema, handler-entry-point, codegen, and checklist sequence.
Maps hosted/testnet configs, handler entry point, deployment namespace mirror, and ABI generator.
Explains why the hosted-build mirrors exist and which namespace and token metadata copies must stay synchronized.

answers.answer: A UI change is incomplete until its changed behavior is exercised in a browser. Run the dashboard on reproducible localhost `127.0.0.1:3210`; verify logged-out behavior in an isolated context or after clearing both Auth.js cookies, including public sign-in and protected-route redirects; then mint a local Auth.js token, set the session cookie, and verify the authenticated identity, protected routes, and controls. Manual `pnpm dev` defaults to the live production Envio Hasura endpoint unless explicitly overridden. Deterministic Playwright runs instead use the local Hasura fixture server as their only GraphQL source and must never target hosted Hasura/Envio.

evidence.supports: Makes browser exercise and both public and authenticated states completion requirements.
Provides the fixed localhost launch and states that manual dev defaults to the live production Envio endpoint.
Defines isolated logged-out verification, cookies, sign-in, and protected-route redirects.
Defines the simulated authenticated-session token/cookie flow and expected UI assertions.
Requires fixture browser tests to use only the local Hasura fixture server in dev and production builds.

answers.answer: Deploy the checked-out, clean, pushed `HEAD` with `pnpm deploy:indexer --yes`, which updates the `envio` trigger branch. Track the explicit 7-character target SHA with `ENVIO_REGISTRATION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=300 pnpm deploy:indexer:status <TARGET_COMMIT> --watch --compact`; registration and all-chain caught-up status are distinct gates, and an unsynced deployment must never be promoted. To prevent agent auto-promotion, pass `--no-promote` (the fail-closed default off `main`) and stop after sync. After an explicit go-live decision, and only after merge for additive changes or an approved compatibility/cutover plan for breaking changes, run `pnpm deploy:indexer:verify <TARGET_COMMIT>`, capture the previous production commit for rollback, then run `pnpm deploy:indexer:promote <TARGET_COMMIT> -y` and verify `prod_status`. Never auto-rollback.

evidence.supports: Explains pre-merge no-promote behavior and compatibility requirements.
Defines clean/pushed preflight, target SHA resolution, and fail-closed non-main promotion policy.
Defines the command that pushes HEAD to the Envio deployment trigger branch and its success signals.
Separates build registration from chain synchronization and defines the registration ceiling.
Gives the foreground status-watch command for the explicit target commit.
Defines the caught-up watch and requires explicit target selection rather than latest-deployment inference.
Provides target-specific diagnostics and forbids promotion before a successful caught-up result.
Requires no-promote runs to stop after sync and print separate later verify/promote commands.
Defines compatibility classification and the mandatory pre-promotion verifier.
Defines the explicit promotion command and production-status confirmation.
Forbids bypassing sync and verification, forbids auto-rollback, and defaults feature branches to no-promote.

answers.answer: `terraform.stacks.json` is the machine-readable ownership and policy registry; do not infer ownership from paths. The `platform` stack is manual-plan and human-approved local-apply. `alerts-rules`, `alerts-delivery`, `aegis`, and `governance-watchdog` are PR-planned and normally applied only after merge to `main` through the `production-infra` GitHub Environment. A registry-marked CI-apply stack can run locally only from a clean `main` checkout whose `HEAD` equals `origin/main`, unless an operator deliberately supplies `--force-local-apply`; the documented safe path remains the environment-gated main apply.

evidence.supports: Names the registry and maps every stack to its ownership and apply policy.
Defines the clean-main local guard, deliberate override, and expected production-infra path.
Requires registry-backed path routing and identifies the stacks with environment-gated main applies.

answers.answer: Apply `docs/pr-checklists/terraform-cloudrun.md`. It requires a reviewed Terraform plan with explicit moved-address protection and no accidental destroys; validates Cloud Run `/health`, memory, API-drift ignores, pinned bootstrap image, and API dependency; requires unique RFC-1035 revision suffixes across both CI and script rollout paths; and finishes with a pre-apply plan review plus a post-apply public `/health` 200 check. Those controls preserve a viable prior revision/image instead of letting Terraform accidentally recreate or revert the service, making rollback and rollout health reviewable.

evidence.supports: Scopes the checklist and requires moved blocks plus a no-destroy plan review.
Defines Cloud Run probe, resource, drift, bootstrap-image, and API-ordering safety checks.
Requires valid unique revisions and verification of both workflow and script rollout paths.
Requires plan inspection before apply and a public health check after rollout.

answers.answer: GitHub Issues are canonical because a Markdown bullet cannot be atomically claimed, has no queryable ready/active state or ownership, and quickly diverges from reality. Issues provide mutually exclusive queue labels, an atomic claim helper, project visibility, and PR integration. Consequently `BACKLOG.md` is transition storage only: after migration, the active task lives in exactly one issue rather than both places, with `source:backlog` preserving provenance.

evidence.supports: Explains Markdown queue failures and decides on atomically claimed, labeled GitHub Issues.
Makes state labels exclusive and requires claims and labeled issues for retained work.
States that labels are authoritative and migrated work must live in one issue, not both trackers.
Defines the source:backlog provenance label for migrated work.

answers.answer: stETH cannot remain event-only because Lido rebases increase balances without transfer events, so a transfer ledger undercounts quiet-period earned yield. The decision establishes a launch-block baseline, samples each tracked wallet every 600 produced Ethereum blocks (about two hours), and rolls those observations into wallet-level daily rows. Thus it is deliberately not a single midnight read: repeated sub-daily observations preserve wallet attribution as principal moves and tolerate a failed later read by skipping that whole batch, while daily storage matches dashboard granularity. Every-block sampling was rejected as needless precision and hosted replay risk.

evidence.supports: Explains rebase invisibility and defines the launch baseline, two-hour cadence, daily rows, and wallet attribution.
Rejects transfer-only inference and every-block polling as undercounting or excessive replay risk.
Defines dashboard daily-row consumption, baseline ordering, failed-batch behavior, and deploy verification.

answers.answer: The garden creates one bounded issue because freshness and deterministic warnings are prompts, not semantic proof; the full corpus is too large for one careful pass, and an unattended model edit can silently delete operating knowledge. A deterministic planner selects at most 10 documents or 15,000 words and the scheduler creates at most one immutable live issue. Claimed or needs-grooming packets block later packets until resolved. The scheduled workflow may mutate Issues only; evidence-backed semantic changes still require the repo skill, a claimed issue, repaired links/catalog, and a normal reviewed PR, never direct scheduled edits or merges.

evidence.supports: Explains the semantic-evidence, context-size, silent-deletion, and duplicate-backlog risks.
Defines deterministic bounded selection, one live immutable issue, and blocked-packet behavior.
Limits workflow mutation authority and retains claimed issue, evidence, PR, links, and catalog gates.
Rejects direct LLM merging, overlapping weekly issues, and full-repository review.
States the bounded claimable consequence and that content pruning remains normal reviewed work.

answers.answer: Use `docs/pr-checklists/stateful-data-ui.md`; it is mandatory for an indexer field rendered by the dashboard. Define the invariant first, then audit schema type/nullability and old-row rollout behavior, every writer and generated artifact, every GraphQL/query/type/rendering reader, and producer/UI fixtures. Explicitly design missing/legacy/error/partial states so degradation is visible. Cover normal render, the relevant state transitions and actions, and failure/legacy-row behavior; static markup alone is insufficient for risky interaction behavior.

evidence.supports: Makes the checklist mandatory for schema, writers, generated queries/types, UI state, and rollout failures.
Requires schema/nullability/rollout checks and consistent writers plus refreshed generated artifacts.
Requires all readers, types, rendering fields, producer/consumer tests, and fixtures to align.
Requires explicit behavior for failed queries, legacy rows, missing metadata, scale, and loading/partial states.
Defines happy-path and state-transition interaction coverage.
Defines degraded-mode tests and rejects static-only assertions for risky interactions.

answers.answer: Use `docs/pr-checklists/ci-workflow-gates.md`. Preserve the ruleset-required `ci` sentinel (and `Code Quality`) as always-running checks: required workflows cannot have `paths` filters, while expensive path selection must happen inside a reporting job. Guard deploy jobs against non-main `workflow_dispatch`; pin every third-party action to a full commit SHA and run the pin checker. Keep `notify-slack-on-main-failure.yml` and its explicit workflow list or documented exclusion set aligned, and run the notifier-coverage checker. After changing a required workflow/check reporter, verify the live rollup with `pnpm pr:ready-state --pr <number> --json`.

evidence.supports: Scopes the checklist and states the always-run, known-branch, pinned-code rule.
Explains the never-ran required-check failure and identifies the required sentinel contexts.
Requires ready-state verification and bans required-workflow path filters while defining advisory routing.
Requires main-branch deploy guards, full-SHA action pins, and the local pin checker.
Defines notifier workflow inclusion/exclusion and structural coverage verification.

answers.answer: The execution gate refuses because package manifests, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, workspace/npm/pnpm configuration, and patches can change package scripts or lifecycle code that the mapped checks would execute. The refusal forces a human/agent to inspect that lifecycle surface before branch-controlled package code runs. After that review, execute `pnpm agent:quality-gate --run --allow-package-script-changes`; the same acknowledgement is required for prewarm, and package-risk acknowledgement becomes part of freshness reuse for the push hook.

evidence.supports: Defines mapped local execution and the package/lock/config/patch refusal plus required flag.
Applies the same reviewed-lifecycle acknowledgement to prewarm and explains freshness reuse.
Makes acknowledgement part of the package-risk reuse key and documents the hook configuration path.

answers.answer: Run `pnpm docs:index --write` to regenerate the catalog and `pnpm docs:index --check` to verify drift, classification, and internal links. Run `pnpm docs:audit --dry-run` to preview the bounded semantic-review packet without editing docs, and `pnpm docs:garden --dry-run --json` to preview the exact weekly garden-issue decision without mutation.

evidence.supports: Lists all four catalog, audit-preview, and garden-decision-preview commands and their effects.

answers.answer: Claim ready work before edits with `pnpm issue:claim --count <n> --agent <name>` (or `--issue <number>` for a specific issue). Move represented work to review with `pnpm issue:review --pr <pr> --issue <issue>`. If work cannot continue or a PR closes unmerged, use `pnpm issue:release --issue <issue>` only when the remaining work is still agent-ready; otherwise use `pnpm issue:release --issue <issue> --needs-grooming`.

evidence.supports: Defines claim, review, and ready-versus-needs-grooming release transitions.
Lists exact commands and explains explicit issue selection and PR issue inference.

answers.answer: The shared final probe is exactly `pnpm pr:ready-state --pr <number> --json`; declare all clear only when its final `ready` value is true for the current head. The Codex description gate requires a `chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]` `+1` created at or after the current-head update lower bound. If Codex is externally blocked and every other required surface is clean, an authorized human may use `/pr-ready-override gate=codex-description-approval head=<full-head-sha> reason=<why this is safe>`; it is head-scoped and overrides only that gate.

evidence.supports: Defines pr:ready-state as the shared source of truth before all-clear.
Defines required review/comment gates and the current-head Codex +1 timing requirement.
Defines the authorized, exact-head break-glass syntax and its narrow limits.
Gives the exact final command and requires ready=true on the current head.

answers.answer: The `alerts-delivery` Terraform stack rooted at `alerts/infra/` owns QuickNode webhooks and their Cloud Function/Slack path, the Sentry-to-Slack bridge, and the Splunk On-Call rotation announcer. Keep it separate from `alerts-rules` because its providers, cadence, state, and blast radius differ. Before changing it: plan before apply and require explicit human approval; treat cosmetic edits as live external API mutations; preserve the chain-scoped QuickNode state-repair assumptions and standalone function lockfiles; run stack validation and `pnpm alerts:infra:plan`, relevant function typecheck/coverage, the webhook-state test when that parser/provisioner changes, the agent quality gate, and the Cloud Run checklist for deploy verification.

evidence.supports: Defines the two-stack boundary, alerts/infra ownership, explicit approval, live APIs, state assumptions, and lockfiles.
Lists validation, plan, package tests, webhook-state regression, quality gate, and Cloud Run verification.

answers.answer: For a manually created worktree or clone, run `./scripts/setup.sh`; Worktrunk-created worktrees invoke the same script automatically as a blocking pre-start hook. It installs dependencies, ensures Playwright Chromium, and runs Envio codegen. Hosted Claude Code instead enters through the repo SessionStart hook and `./scripts/claude-code-web-setup.sh`, gated by `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE`; it recreates the same install/codegen contract in a container that lacks local skills/environment and performs a best-effort, non-fatal Chromium install. Repo-local skill adapters provide ship/babysit behavior without personal skills.

evidence.supports: Defines manual setup, its outputs, and automatic Worktrunk invocation.
Explains local marker-backed setup reuse and platform-specific Playwright behavior.
Defines the hosted SessionStart bootstrap, remote gate, shared setup contract, and non-fatal Chromium step.
Explains repo-local skill adapters and the hosted readiness-watch fallback.

answers.answer: Start from the generated issue: verify both garden markers, all eight Agent Task sections, and the complete planner packet, then claim it before semantic edits. Reproduce selection with `pnpm docs:audit --dry-run --date <selected-for> --lane <lane> --shard <shard>` and compare fingerprint and file list; reconcile catalog drift explicitly. Never overwrite a packet already `needs-grooming`, `agent-active`, or `in-pr`; it remains the one live packet until resolved or closed. If no issue exists, use the current dry-run packet only to bound the audit, then create or claim an Agent Task before editing. For each document, inspect the full document and owning sources, choose exactly one disposition, record concrete evidence, make only supported changes, repair inbound links/indexes, and never delete merely because a document is old; unresolved ownership decisions leave the document unchanged.

evidence.supports: Defines marker validation, claim/blocked behavior, deterministic packet reproduction, fingerprint reconciliation, and no-issue recovery.
Requires full owning-context review, one disposition, concrete evidence, supported changes, and link repair.
Forbids age-only deletion, unverifiable metadata changes, scope expansion, and guessing through ownership conflicts.
Defines catalog/gate verification, disposition evidence, and normal reviewed-PR completion.
